Patch Set 4:

Could probably also remove the assert_spin_locked() in ptlrpc_..._with_imp_locked()?

I wouldn't object to adding __must_hold() for these functions.  Even if we don't run sparse, it at least serves as a visual reminder to the developers of the caller requirements.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> Could probably also remove the assert_spin_locked() in ptlrpc_..._with_imp_locked()?
> 
> I wouldn't object to adding __must_hold() for these functions.  Even if we don't run sparse, it at least serves as a visual reminder to the developers of the caller requirements.

I suppose the visual reminder is a decent idea.  The problem with actually getting us set up for sparse here is that we have to acquires_ and releases_ and the imp_lock is used in a *lot* of places (low hundreds), so it wouldn't be quick work.

Since I don't think we run sparse today (I'm pretty sure we don't - some of our annotations look incomplete), I'll add __must_holds() in the relevant spots.

(1 comment)

Patch Set 11:

There is not an LU, and I didn't open one because I'm not game for building what's required...

So lockdep is a relatively simple (but capable) lock dependency checker, and it requires you create a descriptive model of your locking.  The kernel docs are the best resource I'm aware of (but I've never actually used it):

https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/locking/lockdep-design.txt

It would be a fair bit of work to do.  If someone wants to take it on, I'd be happy to try to support it.  Obviously you'd want to start with doing it in one specific area of code first.

commit.subject: LU-16741 ptlrpc: remove unnecessary asserts
commit.message:
LU-16741 ptlrpc: remove unnecessary asserts

ptlrpc_free_committed is at no risk of being called with
either a null import or the import lock unlocked.  These
don't have a significant cost, but they're not adding much
either.

Removed 'trivial' from this patch to ensure the LU-16741
refactoring series gets at least one full run.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I90d3ddae2cfbb9c748e3ba004982bca3ef465667
